description: Comprehensive UX/UI improvements spanning navigation chrome, destructive operation safety, core token operations, token list navigation, token editing ergonomics, bulk operations, discoverability, validation, sync, and analytics.

userStories.title: Tab Bar Reorganization
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the tab bar to surface only the primary views so that I can focus on the 80% use case without visual noise from utility actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab bar shows exactly four tabs: Tokens | Themes | Sync | Analytics
Import and Export are removed from the tab bar
A '...' overflow menu or gear icon in the toolbar opens a dropdown containing Import and Export actions
Existing Import and Export functionality is fully preserved behind the new entry point
Server Settings is accessible from the overflow menu or within the Sync tab
Active tab has a background fill treatment, not just an underline
Each set tab displays its token count as a badge (e.g. 'primitives 42')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Replace confirm() with In-Plugin Modals
userStories.description: As a designer, I want all destructive confirmations to appear as proper in-plugin dialogs so that I don't get jarring native browser alerts that block the Figma UI.
acceptanceCriteria: All uses of window.confirm() in the plugin are removed
Single-token delete triggers an in-plugin modal showing: token name, and a Delete / Cancel button pair
Group delete modal shows: group name, count of tokens that will be deleted, and 'Delete group (N tokens)' / Cancel
Bulk delete modal shows: count of selected tokens, orphan count, and 'Delete N tokens' / Cancel
Modals are styled consistently with the rest of the plugin UI
Pressing Escape or clicking outside the modal cancels the operation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Shared modal component should be reusable for all subsequent destructive operations

userStories.title: Session Undo for Destructive Operations
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to undo the last destructive operation so that accidental bulk deletes or moves don't permanently destroy my work.
acceptanceCriteria: A single undo slot is maintained in memory per session
The following operations populate the undo slot: delete token, delete group, bulk delete, move to set, bulk rename
After any of these operations completes, a toast or snackbar appears with 'Undo' for at least 8 seconds
Pressing Cmd+Z also triggers undo if the undo slot is populated
Executing undo restores the state to exactly before the destructive operation
Performing a new destructive operation replaces the undo slot (only one level of undo)
Undo slot is cleared on plugin close / session end
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Set Rename
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to rename a token set so that I can fix naming mistakes without recreating the set from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Rename' action is available in the set context menu (right-click and '...' button on set tab)
Renaming opens an inline input pre-filled with the current set name
Name is validated to match [a-zA-Z0-9_-] pattern; invalid characters show an inline error
On confirm: the JSON file on the server is renamed, all theme references to the old name are updated, and all cross-set alias references are updated
If any alias update fails, the rename is rolled back and an error is shown
The set tab reflects the new name immediately after success
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Requires new server endpoint with atomic rename + cascade update

userStories.title: Discoverable Set Context Menu
userStories.description: As a designer, I want a visible menu button on each set tab so that I can discover set-level actions without knowing to right-click.
acceptanceCriteria: Each set tab renders a '...' (kebab) button that is always visible on hover and on the active tab
Clicking '...' opens a context menu with all existing set-level actions: Generate Semantic Tokens, Generate Dark Theme, Adopt Figma File, Rename (US-007), Duplicate, Delete, Set Metadata
Right-click on the tab still works as before (no regression)
The '...' button does not overflow the tab's width
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 for Rename action

userStories.title: Empty State Guidance
userStories.description: As a new user, I want the empty token set to guide me toward my next action so that I don't stare at a blank screen wondering what to do.
acceptanceCriteria: When a set has zero tokens, the main area renders a guided empty state instead of blank space
Empty state includes: a short description of what a token set is, and three CTA buttons: 'Create first token (Cmd+N)', 'Generate color scale', 'Paste tokens from JSON'
Each CTA button triggers the same action as its equivalent in the full UI
Empty state disappears as soon as at least one token exists in the set
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Set-Level Metadata
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to add a description to each token set so that collaborators understand what each set is for.
acceptanceCriteria: Each token set supports an optional $description field stored in the set file or sidecar
Set tab renders a tooltip on hover showing the description when one exists
The Analytics panel's 'by set' breakdown shows the description alongside the set name
A UI affordance (e.g. edit icon in the set context menu) allows setting/editing the description
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Set Tab Visual Treatment & Token Count
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the active set tab to be visually prominent and show its token count so that I always know where I am and how large each set is.
acceptanceCriteria: Active set tab has a filled background (not just underline/text weight change)
Every set tab displays the number of tokens it contains as a small badge or parenthetical
Badge updates immediately when tokens are added or removed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Expand All / Collapse All Groups
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to expand or collapse all token groups at once so that I can quickly navigate large token sets.
acceptanceCriteria: The toolbar row above the token list has 'Expand all' and 'Collapse all' buttons (or a toggle)
'Expand all' opens every group in the current set
'Collapse all' closes every group in the current set
Expanded/collapsed state per group is persisted in sessionStorage keyed by set name
State is restored when switching back to a previously visited set within the same session
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Sort Order in Token List
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to sort the token list by different criteria so that I can find patterns and spot problems more easily.
acceptanceCriteria: A sort control in the toolbar offers: Alphabetical A-Z, Alphabetical Z-A, By type, By usage count, By value, Default (insertion/tree order)
Sorting is applied to the current set's token list without modifying the underlying data
Sort selection persists within the session
Usage count sort is disabled (greyed out) if no scan has been run yet, with a tooltip explaining why
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Filter Persistence Across Set Switches
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want my active type and ref filters to stay applied when I switch between sets so that I can audit all sets without reapplying filters each time.
acceptanceCriteria: Type filter, ref filter, and search query persist when the active set changes
A visual indicator shows that a filter is active while browsing a different set
Filters can still be cleared manually at any time
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Duplicate Value Filter
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to filter the token list to show only tokens that share a raw value with another token so that I can identify missing alias opportunities.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Duplicate values' filter chip is available alongside existing filter options
When active, the list shows only tokens whose raw (unresolved) value is identical to at least one other token in the current set
Tokens are grouped or annotated to show which others share the same value
Filter chip is visually consistent with existing type/ref filter chips
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Copy Token Path / Resolved Value
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to copy a token's CSS variable name or resolved value to my clipboard directly from the token list so that I can use it in code without switching tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering a token row reveals two copy icons in the action area
First icon copies the CSS variable name (e.g. --color-blue-500) to clipboard
Second icon copies the resolved value (e.g. #2563eb) to clipboard
A brief toast/tooltip confirms which value was copied
Copy icons do not shift layout — they appear in the existing action area space
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Jump to Referenced Token from Alias
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to click an alias value to navigate directly to the source token so that I can inspect or edit it without searching manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Alias value chips in the token list and token editor show a subtle '→' icon
Clicking the chip navigates the token list to the source token (scrolls to and highlights it)
If the source token is in a different set, the plugin switches to that set first, then focuses the token
If the source token cannot be found (broken alias), clicking shows an error tooltip instead of navigating
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Easier Token Referencing — Alias Ergonomics
userStories.description: As a designer, I want alias autocomplete to be available everywhere I type a value so that I can reference tokens without switching to the full editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing '{' in any value field (inline edit, quick-edit popover, full TokenEditor) opens the alias autocomplete dropdown
The dropdown filters by token type when the field type is known
Each autocomplete suggestion shows: the token path, a resolved value preview (color swatch or dimension value), and the source set
A dedicated 'alias mode' toggle button on inline-edit rows switches the input to alias mode and immediately opens autocomplete
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dimension / Number Stepper Input
userStories.description: As a designer, I want dimension and number token inputs to behave like a proper number field so that I can nudge values without manually typing each time.
acceptanceCriteria: Dimension and number token value inputs render with up/down stepper arrows
Arrow keys (up/down) increment/decrement by 1; Shift+Arrow by 10
Scrolling the mouse wheel over the input increments/decrements the value
A unit selector (px / rem / %) appears next to the input for dimension tokens
Changing the unit converts the stored value appropriately (e.g. 16px to 1rem)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Richer Previews in Tree/Table View
userStories.description: As a designer, I want token rows to show visual previews for complex types so that I can recognize values at a glance without opening the editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Typography tokens render a small preview string styled with the token's font family, size, and weight
Shadow tokens render a small box with the box-shadow applied
Gradient tokens render a gradient strip
Previews appear in both Tree and Table view modes
Previews degrade gracefully if the value is an unresolved alias (show the alias path instead)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Reference Chain Visualization
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see when a token is a multi-hop alias so that I can understand how deeply nested my references are.
acceptanceCriteria: When a token resolves through 3 or more alias hops, the token row shows a 'via N aliases' badge next to the resolved value swatch
Clicking the badge expands an inline chain view showing each hop: token-A → token-B → token-C → #hex
Collapsing the chain restores the normal row layout
Chains with fewer than 3 hops are not badged (to avoid noise)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Inspect Mode — Canvas-Aware Token Highlighting
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the token list to highlight tokens bound to my selected Figma layer so that I can see the relationship between canvas and token system.
acceptanceCriteria: When a layer is selected on the Figma canvas, the token list highlights or filters to the tokens bound to that layer
A 'Show bound tokens' toggle in the toolbar enables/disables this mode
Hovering a token row in the list triggers a flash/highlight on layers in the canvas that use that token (uses existing select-layer controller message)
If no layer is selected, inspect mode shows a prompt to select a layer
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Selection Inspector Positioning
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the Selection Inspector to always be visible when a layer is selected so that I don't miss it by forgetting to scroll down.
acceptanceCriteria: The Selection Inspector is rendered as a collapsible panel docked at a fixed position (e.g. bottom of the plugin viewport), not inline in the scroll flow
When a Figma layer is selected, the panel automatically expands if collapsed
When no layer is selected, the panel shows a minimal collapsed state with a label 'Select a layer to inspect'
The panel can be manually collapsed by the designer
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Quick Create Token from Selection
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create a token directly from a selected Figma layer's properties so that I can formalize values organically while working on real frames.
acceptanceCriteria: When one or more layers are selected and the SelectionInspector is visible, any unbound property (fill color, stroke color, font family, font size, font weight, dimension) shows a '+' icon next to its value
Clicking '+' opens a token creation form pre-filled with the extracted value and a suggested path based on the active set's naming convention
The designer can edit the suggested name before saving
Saving creates the token in the active set and immediately binds it to the selected layer's property
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-018 docked Selection Inspector

userStories.title: Create Sibling Token
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create a new token in the same group as an existing token so that I don't have to type the full path every time.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-clicking a token row shows a 'Create sibling' action
'Create sibling' opens the token creation form with the group path pre-filled (everything up to the last '.' segment)
Focus is placed on the name field so the designer only needs to type the last path segment
The new token is created in the same set and group as the source token
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Group Management — Rename, Move, Duplicate
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to rename, move, and duplicate entire token groups so that I can restructure my token hierarchy without tedious one-by-one edits.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-clicking a group header shows actions: 'Rename group', 'Move group to set...', 'Duplicate group'
Rename: opens an inline input; on confirm, all tokens within the group have their paths updated and all alias references to those tokens are updated
Move to set: shows a set picker; on confirm, all tokens in the group are moved to the selected set with alias references preserved
Duplicate: creates a copy of the group with a suffix (e.g. -copy) in the same set
All operations are reflected immediately in the UI
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract to Alias Action
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to convert a token with a raw value into an alias referencing a new or existing primitive so that I can refactor hardcoded values into the primitive → semantic hierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-clicking a token with a raw (non-alias) value shows an 'Extract to alias' option
The dialog prompts for: a name for the new primitive token (pre-suggested based on value type and existing naming patterns), or an option to pick an existing token to alias instead
On confirm with a new name: the primitive token is created in the appropriate set, and the current token's value is replaced with an alias reference to the primitive
On confirm with an existing token: the current token's value is replaced with an alias reference to the selected token
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Token References in Gradient Values — Server Resolver
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to use token aliases as color stops in gradient tokens so that semantic color tokens are respected inside gradients across themes.
acceptanceCriteria: The server-side resolver (resolver.ts) expands alias references within gradient stop values before output
Style Dictionary transform pipeline handles alias expansion in gradient stops
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Server-side only first; gradient editor alias mode (plugin side) is a separate concern

userStories.title: Token References in Gradient Values — Editor UI
userStories.description: As a designer, I want each color stop in the gradient editor to support alias mode so that I can reference semantic tokens in gradients.
acceptanceCriteria: The gradient editor in TokenEditor allows each color stop to be toggled between raw color and alias mode
In alias mode, the color stop uses the same alias autocomplete as US-021
On Figma sync, alias-based gradient stops resolve to their current theme value before being pushed as Figma gradient paint values
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-023 (server resolver) and US-021 (alias autocomplete)

userStories.title: Bulk Rename by Name Pattern (Find & Replace for Token Names)
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to find and replace token path segments by pattern so that I can rename a namespace across hundreds of tokens at once.
acceptanceCriteria: A new POST /api/tokens/:set/bulk-rename endpoint is added to the server accepting: find (string or regex pattern), replace (string), and returning: renamed count, skipped (duplicate) paths, and updated alias references
The endpoint renames all matching token paths within the set and updates any alias $value strings that reference the old paths (scan all sets for references, not just the current one)
If any rename would produce a path that already exists, those tokens are excluded from the rename and returned in the skipped list
The Find & Replace panel in the plugin UI accepts a find pattern (plain text or regex) and a replacement string targeting token paths, not values
Preview shows all tokens whose paths will change, with before/after path displayed — computed client-side before the request is sent
If any rename would produce a duplicate path, it is flagged in the preview and blocked before sending
On confirm, the panel calls the new bulk-rename endpoint and refreshes the token list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No bulk-rename endpoint currently exists on the server — it must be built as part of this story. Cross-set alias reference updates require scanning all sets via tokenStore.getSets() + getFlatTokensForSet().

userStories.title: Bulk Alias Creation — Promote to Semantic
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to convert multiple tokens with raw values to aliases in one operation so that I can stop doing tedious one-by-one refactoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting multiple tokens and right-clicking shows 'Convert to aliases'
A modal shows each selected token with its proposed alias match: closest color primitive by delta-E for color tokens, or exact value match for dimensions/numbers
Each row in the modal is individually accept/reject-able
On confirm, selected tokens have their values replaced with alias references to the matched primitives
Uses allTokensFlat with resolved values to compute matches
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Mass Create from Paste
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to paste a JSON fragment or simple name:value lines to create multiple tokens at once so that I can quickly add tokens without using the full Import flow.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Paste tokens' action is accessible from the overflow menu and as a keyboard shortcut
A modal accepts: a JSON fragment in DTCG format, or newline-separated 'name: value' pairs
Parsing errors are shown inline with line numbers
Valid tokens are previewed before creation, showing name, type, and value
On confirm, tokens are created/updated in the currently active set
Conflicts with existing token paths show a warning and allow skip or overwrite per-token
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Command Palette (Cmd+K)
userStories.description: As a power user, I want a fuzzy-search command runner so that I can trigger any action in the plugin without navigating menus.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Cmd+K opens a full-width command palette overlay
The palette accepts free-text fuzzy search across all available commands

Commands include at minimum: Create new token, Find & Replace (values), Find & Replace (names), Generate color scale, Switch to set [name], Go to token..., Export as CSS, Validate tokens, Generate semantic tokens, Generate dark theme, Import, Export
Selecting a command closes the palette and triggers the action
Pressing Escape closes the palette without triggering anything
The '?' keyboard shortcut overlay is removed (command palette supersedes it)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use a simple action registry pattern (map of command id → { label, handler })

userStories.title: Group Presets / Scaffolding Wizard
userStories.description: As a designer starting a new token set, I want to generate a complete group scaffold from a preset so that I don't have to manually create standard token structures.
acceptanceCriteria: When creating a new group (or from the empty state), an option 'Use preset' is available
Available presets: Spacing scale, Border radius scale, Typography scale, Z-index layers, Opacity scale
Each preset shows a preview of the tokens it will generate before confirming
Generated tokens are added to the active set under the specified group path
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Token Linting — Server Config & Rules Engine
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want configurable lint rules stored in server config so that violations can be evaluated server-side.
acceptanceCriteria: Lint configuration is stored in the server config alongside collectionMapping under a lintRules key
Supported rules (each independently enable/disable-able): no-raw-color, require-description, path-pattern, max-alias-depth, no-duplicate-values
A POST /api/tokens/lint endpoint accepts a set name and returns an array of violations with: rule name, token path, severity, and suggested fix type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Server-side rules engine first; plugin UI integration is US-031-plugin

userStories.title: Token Linting — Inline Violation Indicators
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want lint violations to appear inline in the token list so that I catch problems without running a manual validation pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Violations appear inline in the token list as a warning indicator on the affected token row
Lint runs automatically after any token edit (debounced)
Each violation has a quick-fix action where applicable: 'Add missing description' opens the description field, 'Convert to alias' triggers Extract to Alias flow
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Validate All Tokens Panel
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to run a full validation pass and see all problems in one list so that I can address issues systematically.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Validate' button is accessible from the Analytics tab toolbar and from the command palette

Running validation produces a structured list of errors and warnings covering: broken aliases, circular references, tokens missing $type, value/type mismatches, alias chains deeper than the configured max
Each result row shows: severity (error/warning), token path, description of the problem, and a 'Jump to token' link
Results can be filtered by severity
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Color Contrast Checker
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to check WCAG color contrast between tokens directly in the plugin so that I can validate accessibility without leaving my workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Check contrast' action appears in the token editor for color tokens
The action opens a panel letting the designer pick a background color token
The panel shows the contrast ratio and WCAG AA / AAA pass/fail status for normal text, large text, and UI components
The Analytics panel includes a contrast matrix showing foreground/background token pairs with their pass/fail status
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Theme Missing-Coverage Warnings
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see when tokens are not covered by any active set in a theme so that I catch missing values before publishing.
acceptanceCriteria: Each theme card shows a warning badge: 'N tokens have no value in active sets' when coverage gaps exist
Clicking the badge navigates to a filtered list of the uncovered tokens
A token is considered 'uncovered' if it exists in a disabled or excluded set for the theme and no enabled set provides a value for the same path
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Duplicate Color Value Detection
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want to identify color tokens that resolve to the same hex value so that I can eliminate redundant primitives.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Duplicate colors' filter or report is accessible from the Analytics tab
It surfaces groups of color tokens that share an identical resolved hex value (after full alias resolution)
Each group shows all token paths resolving to that value
A 'Deduplicate' action allows promoting one as the canonical primitive and converting the others to aliases
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Color Scale Lightness Curve Inspector
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see the perceptual lightness distribution of a generated color scale so that I can spot uneven steps before publishing.
acceptanceCriteria: After generating a color scale, a small sparkline chart appears below the generated tokens showing the L* (Oklch lightness) value for each step
Steps with disproportionately large or small lightness jumps are visually highlighted (e.g. red/orange dot on the sparkline)
The chart is non-interactive in the minimum implementation (display only)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Two-Way Variable Sync with Unified Diff
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see local and Figma-side changes together in one view so that I can make an informed decision about sync direction per token.
acceptanceCriteria: A single 'Sync' action in the Sync tab computes both computeFigmaDiff and computePullDiff and merges the results into one diff view
Diff rows are categorized: local-only changes, Figma-only changes, conflicts (both sides changed)
Each row shows a direction arrow toggle: push local to Figma, pull Figma to local, or skip
Confirming applies all selected direction choices in a single operation
The existing separate push and pull flows are preserved as quick-action shortcuts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Token identity key must handle renamed tokens correctly

userStories.title: Partial Sync by Group
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to sync a single token group to Figma instead of the entire set so that iterative workflows are faster and safer.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-clicking a group header shows 'Sync this group to Figma'
The action pushes only the tokens within that group to Figma variables, leaving other groups untouched
A confirmation shows the count of tokens that will be synced
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Variable Scope Management UI
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to configure the Figma variable scopes for each token directly in the plugin so that I control where variables appear in Figma's native variable picker.
acceptanceCriteria: The full TokenEditor includes a 'Scopes' section listing available Figma variable scopes for the token's type
Table view includes an optional 'Scopes' column (toggleable)
Scope selections are stored in the token data and respected during syncToFigma via scopeOverrides
A group-level scope editor allows setting the same scopes for all tokens in a group at once
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Sync Status Per-Row Indicator
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to see at a glance which tokens are out of sync with Figma without having to enable a filter so that I notice drift proactively.
acceptanceCriteria: Each token row shows a subtle indicator dot (orange for 'changed since last sync', red for 'diverged') when the token's sync state is non-clean
The indicator is always-on for tokens with non-clean sync state (no filter required)
Hovering the indicator shows a tooltip: 'Changed locally since last sync' or 'Diverged — both local and Figma have changes'
Tokens with clean sync state show no indicator
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Publish Readiness Checklist
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want a pre-publish checklist that validates Figma variable state so that I can confidently publish a Figma library.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Publish Readiness' panel is accessible from the Sync tab or as a command palette action
Checklist categories with pass/fail status: All tokens have corresponding Figma variables, Scopes are set for every variable, Descriptions are populated for every variable, No orphan Figma variables
Each failed check shows a count of affected items and a 'Fix' action where automation is possible
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Figma Component Token Coverage Map
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want a report showing which Figma components use tokens vs hardcoded values so that I can track tokenization progress.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Component Coverage' report is accessible from the Analytics tab
Running the scan walks all component nodes in the current Figma file and checks bound variables vs raw fills/strokes/text styles
Report shows: total component count, percentage tokenized, and a list of untokenized components with their hardcoded property counts
Clicking an untokenized component in the list selects it on the Figma canvas
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Token Documentation / Style Guide Generation
userStories.description: As a design system maintainer, I want auto-generated visual style guide pages on the server's /docs route so that I can share the token system with stakeholders.
acceptanceCriteria: The server's /docs route renders auto-generated HTML pages
Color tokens are displayed as palette swatches with hex values and contrast ratios
Typography tokens render specimen text styled with the actual font/size/weight values
Spacing tokens render a visual scale (boxes of increasing size)
Pages are shareable via direct URL with no authentication required (configurable)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Filter Bar Always-On Type Filter Chip
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to see the Type filter chip without having to interact with the search bar first so that I discover filtering exists.
acceptanceCriteria: The Type filter chip is always visible in the filter bar regardless of search bar focus or active filter state
Other filter chips (ref type, deprecated, missing desc) continue to appear on demand or when active
The always-on chip does not cause layout overflow in the filter bar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Semantic Generation Discoverability
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to discover 'Generate Semantic Tokens' and 'Generate Dark Theme' as prominent actions so that I find these high-value features without stumbling into a right-click context menu.
acceptanceCriteria: After completing the starter wizard flow, the completion screen presents 'Generate Semantic Tokens' and 'Generate Dark Theme' as primary CTA buttons
Empty-set states include these as secondary actions if a primitives set exists
The actions remain available in the set context menu as before
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (empty state) and US-028 (set context menu)

userStories.title: Gradient Token Editor
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create and edit gradient tokens in the plugin so that I can manage gradient values alongside the rest of my design tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: TokenEditor renders a GradientEditor component when tokenType === 'gradient'
GradientEditor displays a list of color stops, each with a color picker + hex input and a position (0–1) input
Stops can be added ('+' button) and removed (×) — minimum 2 stops enforced
A live gradient preview strip above the stop list shows the linear gradient as stops are edited
The default new gradient has two stops: #000000 at 0 and #ffffff at 1
Stops are stored in DTCG format: array of { color: string, position: number }
The 'gradient' type is selectable in the type dropdown when creating a new token
TokenList renders a gradient strip preview for gradient tokens (consistent with US-015 richer previews)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: US-023b (alias mode per stop) depends on this story. Keep the editor self-contained as a GradientEditor sub-component in TokenEditor.tsx.

acceptanceCriteria: TokenEditor renders a DurationEditor when tokenType === 'duration'
DurationEditor shows a number input and a unit selector (ms / s); stored as { value: number, unit: 'ms' | 's' }
A row of quick-select preset chips is shown: 100ms, 150ms, 200ms, 300ms, 500ms
The 'duration' type is selectable in the type dropdown when creating a new token
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Standalone FontFamily & FontWeight Token Editors
userStories.description: As a designer, I want to create standalone fontFamily and fontWeight tokens (separate from typography composites) so that I can build a primitive font scale that typography tokens reference.
acceptanceCriteria: TokenEditor renders a FontFamilyEditor when tokenType === 'fontFamily'
FontFamilyEditor is a text input storing a string (e.g. 'Inter', 'Georgia, serif')
TokenEditor renders a FontWeightEditor when tokenType === 'fontWeight'
FontWeightEditor is a select with numeric weight options (100–900) plus named labels (Thin, Light, Regular, Medium, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold, Black); stored as a number
Both types are selectable in the type dropdown when creating a new token
TokenList renders a meaningful inline preview: font name rendered in that font for fontFamily, sample text at the stored weight for fontWeight
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

acceptanceCriteria: TokenEditor renders a StrokeStyleEditor when tokenType === 'strokeStyle'
StrokeStyleEditor is a select of keyword values: solid, dashed, dotted, double, groove, ridge, outset, inset; stored as a string
The 'strokeStyle' type is selectable in the type dropdown when creating a new token

userStories.title: Bug Sweep — Error Handling, Edge Cases, and Broken States
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a systematic pass over the codebase to find and fix real bugs so that the plugin behaves correctly under edge cases and adverse conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all fetch() calls in the plugin UI: every call must handle non-ok responses and network failures with a visible error message — no silent failures
SSE disconnect (useServerConnection) must show a clear 'Disconnected' state and attempt reconnection with exponential backoff; verify the current behavior is correct
Token creation must reject empty or whitespace-only token paths with an inline validation error before sending to the server
Switching sets while a token fetch is in-flight must not cause a stale-update race (cancel or ignore in-flight requests for the previous set)
The undo slot (US-006) must not restore state if the server rejected the original operation — verify the current undo implementation checks for this
Alias resolution in resolveAlias.ts must not infinitely loop on circular references — verify there is a cycle guard and add one if missing
Any console.error or unhandled promise rejection visible in the browser during normal use must be fixed

Read through all source files in all packages looking for logic bugs, unsafe assumptions, missing null checks, incorrect conditionals, and off-by-one errors — fix anything found regardless of whether it surfaces as a visible error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Both approaches are required: read the code manually for logic bugs, AND run dev-browser to catch runtime errors. Do not limit the sweep to observed symptoms.

userStories.title: Dead Code and Cleanup Pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all unreachable code, unused exports, and vestigial patterns removed so that the codebase stays lean and readable.
acceptanceCriteria: Run TypeScript compiler with noUnusedLocals and noUnusedParameters (or equivalent) and fix all reported unused variables and parameters
Remove any imports that are no longer used anywhere in the codebase
Remove any components, hooks, or utility functions that are defined but never imported anywhere
Remove any TODO/FIXME comments that have been resolved; leave ones that represent real known gaps
Remove any commented-out blocks of code (not comments explaining logic, but commented-out implementation)
Ensure no duplicate type definitions exist across packages — if a type is defined in core and re-declared locally, use the core one
Typecheck passes
No new lint warnings introduced
userStories.notes: Sweep the entire codebase — all packages. Do not limit to files changed on this branch.

userStories.title: UX Polish — Feedback, Clarity, and Ease of Learning
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want the plugin to communicate clearly at every step so that I understand what is happening, what went wrong, and what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: Every destructive or slow action (delete, sync, push, export) must show a loading state on the triggering button for the full duration of the operation
Every error message shown to the user must be human-readable — no raw JavaScript error strings, fetch status codes, or JSON blobs exposed directly
Empty states: every panel that can be empty (no sets, no tokens, no themes, no git repo, no Figma selection) must show a sentence explaining why it is empty and what the user should do
All icon-only buttons must have a title or aria-label attribute so they are identifiable on hover and accessible to screen readers
Truncated token paths in the list must show the full path in a tooltip (title attribute is sufficient)
Success confirmations: operations that complete silently (e.g. token saved, theme updated) must show at minimum the existing notify toast so users know the action succeeded
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Focus on what a new user sees on their first run-through, not power-user flows.

userStories.title: Quality of Life — Persistence, Defaults, and Friction Reduction
userStories.description: As a returning user, I want the plugin to remember my context and reduce repetitive interactions so that daily use feels fast and frictionless.
acceptanceCriteria: Last active set tab is persisted in localStorage and restored on plugin open
Last active main tab (Tokens / Themes / Sync / Analytics) is persisted in localStorage and restored on plugin open
Server URL is persisted in localStorage (already done via useServerConnection — verify it survives plugin close/reopen)
The token creation form remembers the last-used token type and pre-selects it for the next new token within the same session
Clicking the token type badge in the token list filters to that type (one-click filter shortcut)
Pressing Escape while the TokenEditor is open navigates back to the token list without requiring the Cancel button
Pressing Enter while focused on the commit message input in SyncPanel submits the commit (already partially implemented — verify it works and covers the edge case of an empty message)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Prefer localStorage over sessionStorage for anything that should survive plugin close. Use sessionStorage only for within-session state like expand/collapse.
